label: New — Visual Lens (Analyse)
items: Plant Doctor gets a new headline action: Analyse. One photo, one tap — and you get identification, a health & light read, pruning method, propagation steps, edibility & ripeness check, plus disease and pest workups if anything's wrong.

Suggested Tasks are generated in the same payload. Review the pre-checked list at the bottom of the result, deselect anything you don't want, and add the rest to your calendar with a single tap — watering, pruning, harvesting reminders, treatment schedules, and propagation prompts all use the same task system you already know.
Analyse uses your area context (sunlight, soil, drainage, recent care) and the local weather to make recommendations that actually fit your garden — not generic plant-care advice.

label: Behind the scenes
items: Plant Doctor now reuses one shared environmental-context helper across Diagnose and Analyse, so both actions see the same area, lux, companion plants, recent tasks, and current weather when making their call.
Suggested Tasks from Analyse use the exact same write path the AI Chat already uses for its task suggestions — no new code paths, no divergent behaviour.
